evals.name: outline-to-deck
evals.prompt:
I have this markdown outline for a short talk I'm giving next week. Turn it into an HTML presentation I can present from a browser, A4 landscape format, clean minimal style. Keep my wording verbatim — don't paraphrase.

```
## Slide 1 — Title
- Boring tools win
- Why your AI orchestrator is just tmux underneath
- Daisy Lee · DevConf · 2026

## Slide 2 — A confession
- Last week I was juggling four agents and lost track of which one was on which branch
- I had 12 terminal windows open, all called "Terminal"
- This talk is about lifting that limit

> *Speaker note:* tell it as a story, not a complaint. Pause after "lifting that limit".

## Slide 3 — What people think tmux is
- Splits, tabs, hotkeys
- "I have iTerm2, why bother?"
- This is a misunderstanding — and it's the reason most people never adopt it

## Slide 4 — What tmux actually is
- A tiny operating system for terminal processes
- Sessions live in a server; your terminal is just a window onto them
- Decoupling sessions from the emulator is the whole game

![[architecture.png]]

## Slide 5 — The combo
- One repo. Many worktrees. Many tmux sessions.
- Detach, walk away, the work continues

## Slide 6 — What I recommend
- Spend an afternoon
- Six tmux commands, three worktree commands
- Try it tomorrow on real work
```
evals.expected_output: A single self-contained HTML file using the fixed-canvas pattern (--slide-w / --slide-h CSS variables), with 6 sections of class='slide', preserving the user's bullet wording verbatim, with the speaker note in a data-notes attribute on slide 2, and a reference to architecture.png on slide 4.

evals.name: topic-only-brief
evals.prompt: I'm giving a 10-minute lightning talk at the data team's offsite next month about why our team should adopt feature flags. Could you build me a presentation? About 8 slides feels right. The audience is mid-level data engineers — they know the basics but probably haven't used flags day-to-day. I want it to feel polished but not corporate. Conference talk vibe.
evals.expected_output: Either: (a) the skill asks Phase 1 capture-intent questions before generating (preferred), OR (b) generates a complete 8-slide HTML deck on the topic with a clear narrative arc, fixed-canvas layout, and conference-appropriate styling.

evals.name: enhance-existing-deck
evals.prompt:
I have an existing HTML presentation at presentation.html. I need to add three new slides at the end about the rollout timeline:

1. Phase 1 — internal beta (next 2 weeks)
2. Phase 2 — limited GA (March)
3. Phase 3 — full launch (April)

Make them match the existing style — don't change the rest of the deck. Each slide should have one heading and 3-4 short bullets.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads the existing HTML, identifies its style/canvas/layout conventions, and appends three new <section class='slide'> blocks that match — same canvas dimensions, same color palette, same layout pattern. Does not modify slides that already exist.
